criteria: io_uring instance is initialized via io_uring_setup syscall with configurable queue depth, and SQ/CQ rings are correctly mmap'd using IORING_OFF_SQ_RING, IORING_OFF_SQES, and IORING_OFF_CQ_RING offsets
SQ and CQ ring pointer arithmetic uses ring_mask (not modulo) for index wrapping; application correctly accesses SQE array through sq_array indirection
All ring tail updates use io_uring_smp_store_release before io_uring_enter; all head reads use io_uring_smp_load_acquire before accessing CQE data
Batched submission demonstrated with 10+ SQEs prepared before single io_uring_enter call; all corresponding CQEs harvested and processed
Fixed buffers are registered via IORING_REGISTER_BUFFERS with page-aligned iovecs; IORING_OP_READ_FIXED/WRITE_FIXED correctly specify buf_index instead of addr
Provided buffer ring is registered via IORING_REGISTER_PBUF_RING with valid io_uring_buf_reg structure; SQEs use IOSQE_BUFFER_SELECT flag and correct buf_group ID
CQE res field is checked for negative errno; EAGAIN triggers retry scheduling, ECANCELED triggers cleanup, ENOBUFS triggers buffer ring replenishment
CQ overflow is detected by checking sq_off.dropped counter; application handles or prevents overflow scenarios
Cleanup sequence: unregister all registered resources (IORING_UNREGISTER_BUFFERS, IORING_UNREGISTER_PBUF_RING), munmap all ring regions, close ring fd
File server serves read requests using IORING_OP_READ with correct file descriptor and explicit offset parameters (not relying on file position)
File descriptors are registered with IORING_REGISTER_FILES and SQEs use IOSQE_FIXED_FILE flag with the registered index (not raw fd numbers)
Short reads (CQE res > 0 but < requested bytes) are detected and handled by resubmitting a read for remaining bytes at updated offset with adjusted buffer pointer
Server maintains at least 64 concurrent in-flight read operations with correct buffer-to-request association tracked via user_data field
Direct I/O (O_DIRECT) variant is implemented with buffers allocated via posix_memalign or aligned_alloc to 4096-byte boundaries and file offsets aligned to filesystem block size
Benchmark suite demonstrates at least 2x throughput improvement over synchronous pread for 64+ concurrent 4KB random reads on SSD, with syscall count reduced by >95%
Buffer lifetime is guaranteed via ownership tracking: buffers are marked in_use when submitted, released only after CQE harvested, and never reassigned while async operation is pending
Error handling distinguishes between negative CQE res values (errors), zero (EOF), and positive short reads, with appropriate handling for each case
Registered file table supports dynamic updates via IORING_REGISTER_FILES_UPDATE for adding and removing files without re-registering entire table
CQE overflow is detected and handled by checking the SQ ring's dropped counter or IORING_SQ_CQ_OVERFLOW flag
io_uring instance is initialized with io_uring_setup syscall and SQ/CQ rings are mmap'd into userspace with correct offsets (IORING_OFF_SQ_RING, IORING_OFF_SQES, IORING_OFF_CQ_RING)
SQ and CQ sizes are configured correctly with CQ at least 2x SQ size via kernel default or explicit IORING_SETUP_CQSIZE flag
Memory barriers (io_uring_smp_store_release for tail, io_uring_smp_load_acquire for head) are correctly placed between ring index updates and data access
Batched submission demonstrated by preparing 10+ SQEs and submitting with single io_uring_enter call returning the submitted count
All prepared SQEs result in harvested CQEs verified by iterating through completion queue until count matches submission
Fixed buffers are registered with IORING_REGISTER_BUFFERS using page-aligned addresses (posix_memalign or aligned_alloc with 4096 alignment)
IORING_OP_READ_FIXED and IORING_OP_WRITE_FIXED use registered buffer indices correctly with buf_index field set
Provided buffer ring is set up with IORING_REGISTER_PBUF_RING with valid io_uring_buf_reg structure containing ring_addr, ring_entries, and bgid
Operations using provided buffers specify IOSQE_BUFFER_SELECT flag and buf_group field in SQE
CQE error handling checks res field for negative values and correctly maps -res to errno for error messages
EAGAIN, ECANCELED, and EBUSY errors are handled with appropriate retry or cleanup logic
CQ overflow is detected by checking the dropped counter in SQ ring (sq_off.dropped offset) with non-zero value indicating lost completions
All registered resources (IORING_UNREGISTER_BUFFERS, IORING_UNREGISTER_FILES, IORING_UNREGISTER_PBUF_RING) are unregistered during cleanup before munmap and close
SQE preparation clears the structure (memset to zero) before filling fields to prevent information leakage from padding bytes
Buffer ownership is tracked with in_use or similar flag preventing buffer reuse while referenced by in-flight SQE
